<<Course Structure and How to Get the Most Out of It>>

*highlighted section*  
**1. Understand the Course Layout**  
Most courses follow a structured format, often divided into modules, units, or weeks. Take time to review the syllabus and identify key topics, deadlines, and assessment methods. For example, [module 1] might focus on [topic A], while [module 2] covers [topic B]. Knowing the layout helps you prioritize your time and avoid last-minute stress.

*highlighted section*  
**2. Set Clear Goals and Prioritize Tasks**  
Break down larger assignments into smaller, manageable tasks. Use tools like [planner] or [calendar] to schedule your work. For instance, if [assignment X] is due in [weeks], allocate [hours] per week to complete it. This ensures you're consistently progressing and not overwhelmed by [final project].

*highlighted section*  
**3. Engage Actively and Seek Feedback**  
Don’t just passively consume content. Participate in [discussion forums], ask questions in [office hours], and seek feedback on [drafts]. Engaging with [instructors] and [peers] can clarify doubts and improve your understanding of [complex concepts]. Always aim to [improve] your skills through [practice].
